_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Comprehensive medical rehabilitation/ : Comprehensive medical rehabilitation_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Comprehensive medical rehabilitation_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
min_doctor_qualification: BOT (Under supervision of Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation specialist)
clinical_indications: Patient diagnosed with special learning disability requiring medical rehabilitation, which may include neurorehabilitation, physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy, social skill training, and psychotherapy.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
Details past history with treatment records
Electroencephalogram (EEG) report in intellectual and learning disability

claim.label: Indoor case papers with treatment given details

ppd_preauth.q: Are all the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, planned line of treatment submitted?
Is Details patient past history & treatment records submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and line of treatment submitted?
Is the discharge summary submitted with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes, evaluation findings, or planned line of treatment not submitted or incomplete.
X-Ray/CT/MRI report not submitted or incomplete.
Patient's past history or treatment records not submitted or incomplete.
Electroencephalogram (EEG) report for intellectual and learning disability not submitted or incomplete.
Detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and line of treatment not submitted or incomplete.
Discharge summary not submitted, or lacking follow-up advice.
Patient's past history or diagnostic report does not suggest rehabilitation treatment.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB (General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine, Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation), DM/DNB (Neurology)
clinical_key_pointers: Rehabilitation is defined as "a set of interventions designed to optimize functioning and reduce disability in individuals with health conditions in interaction with their environment.
Rehabilitation is an essential part of universal health coverage along with promotion of good health, prevention of disease, treatment and palliative care.

Rehabilitation helps a child, adult or older person to be as independent as possible in everyday activities and enables participation in education, work, recreation and meaningful life roles such as taking care of family.
